danger_signals.meaning: Environmental damage or instant death zones

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Darkman head sprite (documented in unused graphics)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Different environmental layouts across selectable sublevels (accessible via stage select)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy placement and difficulty scaling across stage progression

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dedicated boss arena design (boss shooting events selectable)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss-specific attack patterns and enemy behavior distinct from normal stages

environmental_storytelling.example: Sublevel design communicates progression through the narrative (multiple selectable sublevels suggest different locations/scenarios from source material)
Boss shooting events as separate selectable content establish key confrontation moments

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Distinct attack patterns in dedicated boss shooting events

notes: Source material documents development/technical information only; no visual communication patterns explicitly detailed. Analysis limited by tier T3 data availability. Game is based on 1991 film 'Darkman,' suggesting theme of hero in disguise/dual identity may be reflected in sprite work (Darkman head asset noted), but visual implementation not documented in available source. Unused graphics (Darkman head, extra life icon) suggest planned but unimplemented visual communication systems.
